(Dating based on Ikko-ikki inscription tiles is approximate; some sources propose Tenshō 8 ({{ill|1580 AD|ja|1580th year }} {{ill|July 5|ja|7月5日}} )). * {{ill|Kansei|ja|Kansei}} 2nd Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1790 AD|ja|1790th year }} {{ill|July 6|ja|7月6日}} ) – The Kansei Prohibition on Heterodox Learning was enacted, with {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信}} banning the teaching of subjects other than {{ill|Neo-Confucianism|ja|朱子学}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂}} .
== Births == * {{ill|Tenryaku|ja|天暦}} 4th Year ([[Category:Lunar calendar translation marker]] {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|950 AD|ja|950th year }} {{ill|June 12|ja|6月12日}} ) – {{ill|Emperor Reisō|ja|冷泉天皇}} , the 63rd {{ill|Emperor|ja|天皇}} (d. {{ill|1011 AD|ja|1011th year }} ). * {{ill|Gennin|ja|元永}} 1st Year ([[Category:Lunar calendar translation marker]] {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1118 AD|ja|1118th year }} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}} ) – {{ill|Chiba Tsunetane|ja|千葉常胤}} , a military commander and protector (shugo) of {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国|lt=Shimōsa}} (d. {{ill|1201 AD|ja|1201st year }} ). * {{ill|Shōhō|ja|正保}} 1st Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1644 AD|ja|1644th year }} {{ill|June 28|ja|6月28日}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunashige|ja|徳川綱重}} , the first lord of the {{ill|Kōfu Domain|ja|甲府藩}} , the third son of {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} , and the father of {{ill|Tokugawa Ienobu|ja|徳川家宣}} (d. {{ill|1678 AD|ja|1678th year }} ). * {{ill|Kōka|ja|弘化}} 3rd Year (leap May) ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1846 AD|ja|1846th year }} {{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa Iemoto|ja|徳川家茂}} , the 14th shogun of the {{ill|Edo Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}} (d. {{ill|1866 AD|ja|1866th year }} ). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 4th Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1871 AD|ja|1871st year }} {{ill|July 11|ja|7月11日}} ) – {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉}} , a historian (d. {{ill|1939 AD|ja|1939th year }} ).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 13th Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1636 AD|ja|1636th year }} {{ill|June 27|ja|6月27日}} ) – {{ill|Date Masamune|ja|伊達政宗}} , a military commander and the first lord of the {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}} (born {{ill|1567 AD|ja|1567th year }} ).
== Commemorations == *
[[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category: tier 1]] [[ja:年表]] == See Also ==

[[sn:May 24 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 5월 24일]] [[cdo:5 nguŏk 24 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:5月24日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 5월 24일]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 24 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月廿四]] [[zh:五月廿四]] [[en:draft:May 24 (Lunisolar)]]
{{Lunar May}} '''May 24''' is the 24th day of month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May}} the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Butsumetsu|ja|仏滅}} .
== Events == * {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} 3rd Year ([[Category:Lunar calendar translation marker]] {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|699 AD|ja|699th year }} {{ill|June 26|ja|6月26日}} ) – The founder of Shugendō, {{ill|En no Ozunu|ja|役行者|lt=En no Ozunu|lt_ja=役小角}} , was exiled to {{ill|Izu Province|ja|Izu Province|lt=Izu|lt_ja=伊豆}} due to slander from his disciples. * {{ill|Bunji|ja|文治}} 1st Year ([[Category:Lunar calendar translation marker]] {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1185 AD|ja|1185th year }} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日}} ) – To appease {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} , {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|lt=Yoshitsune|lt_ja=義経}} , who was en route to Kamakura, was detained at {{ill|Koshigoe|ja|腰越}} , and thus entrusted a letter (the {{ill|Koshigoe Letter|ja|腰越状}} ) to {{ill|Ōe Hiromoto|ja|大江広元}} . * {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era}} 4th Year ([[Category:Lunar calendar translation marker]] {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1576 AD|ja|1576th year }} {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日}} ) – {{ill|Maeda Toshiie|ja|Maeda Toshiie}} executed approximately 1,000 captives of the {{ill|Echizen Ikko-ikki|ja|越前一向一揆}} , subjecting them to crucifixion and boiling. (Dating based on Ikko-ikki inscription tiles is approximate; some sources propose Tenshō 8 ({{ill|1580 AD|ja|1580th year }} {{ill|July 5|ja|7月5日}} )). * {{ill|Kansei|ja|Kansei}} 2nd Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1790 AD|ja|1790th year }} {{ill|July 6|ja|7月6日}} ) – The Kansei Prohibition on Heterodox Learning was enacted, with {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信}} banning the teaching of subjects other than {{ill|Neo-Confucianism|ja|朱子学}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂}} .
== Births == * {{ill|Tenryaku|ja|天暦}} 4th Year ([[Category:Lunar calendar translation marker]] {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|950 AD|ja|950th year }} {{ill|June 12|ja|6月12日}} ) – {{ill|Emperor Reisō|ja|冷泉天皇}} , the 63rd {{ill|Emperor|ja|天皇}} (d. {{ill|1011 AD|ja|1011th year }} ). * {{ill|Gennin|ja|元永}} 1st Year ([[Category:Lunar calendar translation marker]] {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1118 AD|ja|1118th year }} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}} ) – {{ill|Chiba Tsunetane|ja|千葉常胤}} , a military commander and protector (shugo) of {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国|lt=Shimōsa}} (d. {{ill|1201 AD|ja|1201st year }} ). * {{ill|Shōhō|ja|正保}} 1st Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1644 AD|ja|1644th year }} {{ill|June 28|ja|6月28日}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunashige|ja|徳川綱重}} , the first lord of the {{ill|Kōfu Domain|ja|甲府藩}} , the third son of {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} , and the father of {{ill|Tokugawa Ienobu|ja|徳川家宣}} (d. {{ill|1678 AD|ja|1678th year }} ). * {{ill|Kōka|ja|弘化}} 3rd Year (leap May) ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1846 AD|ja|1846th year }} {{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa Iemoto|ja|徳川家茂}} , the 14th shogun of the {{ill|Edo Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}} (d. {{ill|1866 AD|ja|1866th year }} ). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 4th Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1871 AD|ja|1871st year }} {{ill|July 11|ja|7月11日}} ) – {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉}} , a historian (d. {{ill|1939 AD|ja|1939th year }} ).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 13th Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1636 AD|ja|1636th year }} {{ill|June 27|ja|6月27日}} ) – {{ill|Date Masamune|ja|伊達政宗}} , a military commander and the first lord of the {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}} (born {{ill|1567 AD|ja|1567th year }} ).

[[sn:May 24 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 5월 24일]] [[cdo:5 nguŏk 24 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:5月24日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 5월 24일]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 24 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月廿四]] [[zh:五月廿四]] [[en:draft:May 24 (Lunisolar)]]
{{Lunar May}} '''May 24''' is the 24th day of month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May}} the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Butsumetsu|ja|仏滅}} .
== Events == * {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} 3rd Year ([[Category:Lunar calendar translation marker]] {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|699 AD|ja|699th year }} {{ill|June 26|ja|6月26日}} ) – The founder of Shugendō, {{ill|En no Ozunu|ja|役行者|lt=En no Ozunu|lt_ja=役小角}} , was exiled to {{ill|Izu Province|ja|Izu Province|lt=Izu|lt_ja=伊豆}} due to slander from his disciples. * {{ill|Bunji|ja|文治}} 1st Year ([[Category:Lunar calendar translation marker]] {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1185 AD|ja|1185th year }} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日}} ) – To appease {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} , {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|lt=Yoshitsune|lt_ja=義経}} , who was en route to Kamakura, was detained at {{ill|Koshigoe|ja|腰越}} , and thus entrusted a letter (the {{ill|Koshigoe Letter|ja|腰越状}} ) to {{ill|Ōe Hiromoto|ja|大江広元}} . * {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era}} 4th Year ([[Category:Lunar calendar translation marker]] {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1576 AD|ja|1576th year }} {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日}} ) – {{ill|Maeda Toshiie|ja|Maeda Toshiie}} executed approximately 1,000 captives of the {{ill|Echizen Ikko-ikki|ja|越前一向一揆}} , subjecting them to crucifixion and boiling. (Dating based on Ikko-ikki inscription tiles is approximate; some sources propose Tenshō 8 ({{ill|1580 AD|ja|1580th year }} {{ill|July 5|ja|7月5日}} )). * {{ill|Kansei|ja|Kansei}} 2nd Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1790 AD|ja|1790th year }} {{ill|July 6|ja|7月6日}} ) – The Kansei Prohibition on Heterodox Learning was enacted, with {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信}} banning the teaching of subjects other than {{ill|Neo-Confucianism|ja|朱子学}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂}} .
== Births == * {{ill|Tenryaku|ja|天暦}} 4th Year ([[Category:Lunar calendar translation marker]] {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|950 AD|ja|950th year }} {{ill|June 12|ja|6月12日}} ) – {{ill|Emperor Reisō|ja|冷泉天皇}} , the 63rd {{ill|Emperor|ja|天皇}} (d. {{ill|1011 AD|ja|1011th year }} ). * {{ill|Gennin|ja|元永}} 1st Year ([[Category:Lunar calendar translation marker]] {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1118 AD|ja|1118th year }} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}} ) – {{ill|Chiba Tsunetane|ja|千葉常胤}} , a military commander and protector (shugo) of {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国|lt=Shimōsa}} (d. {{ill|1201 AD|ja|1201st year }} ). * {{ill|Shōhō|ja|正保}} 1st Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1644 AD|ja|1644th year }} {{ill|June 28|ja|6月28日}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunashige|ja|徳川綱重}} , the first lord of the {{ill|Kōfu Domain|ja|甲府藩}} , the third son of {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} , and the father of {{ill|Tokugawa Ienobu|ja|徳川家宣}} (d. {{ill|1678 AD|ja|1678th year }} ). * {{ill|Kōka|ja|弘化}} 3rd Year (leap May) ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1846 AD|ja|1846th year }} {{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa Iemoto|ja|徳川家茂}} , the 14th shogun of the {{ill|Edo Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}} (d. {{ill|1866 AD|ja|1866th year }} ). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 4th Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1871 AD|ja|1871st year }} {{ill|July 11|ja|7月11日}} ) – {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉}} , a historian (d. {{ill|1939 AD|ja|1939th year }} ).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 13th Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1636 AD|ja|1636th year }} {{ill|June 27|ja|6月27日}} ) – {{ill|Date Masamune|ja|伊達政宗}} , a military commander and the first lord of the {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}} (born {{ill|1567 AD|ja|1567th year }} ).
== Commemorations == *
[[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category: tier 1]] [[ja:年表]]

[[sn:May 24 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 5월 24일]] [[cdo:5 nguŏk 24 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:5月24日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 5월 24일]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 24 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月廿四]] [[zh:五月廿四]] [[en:draft:May 24 (Lunisolar)]]

(Dating based on Ikko-ikki inscription tiles is approximate; some sources propose Tenshō 8 ({{ill|1580 AD|ja|1580th year }} {{ill|July 5|ja|7月5日}} )). * {{ill|Kansei|ja|Kansei}} 2nd Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1790 AD|ja|1790th year }} {{ill|July 6|ja|7月6日}} ) – The Kansei Prohibition on Heterodox Learning was enacted, with {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信}} banning the teaching of subjects other than {{ill|Neo-Confucianism|ja|朱子学}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂}} .
== Births == * {{ill|Tenryaku|ja|天暦}} 4th Year ([[Category:Lunar calendar translation marker]] {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|950 AD|ja|950th year }} {{ill|June 12|ja|6月12日}} ) – {{ill|Emperor Reisō|ja|冷泉天皇}} , the 63rd {{ill|Emperor|ja|天皇}} (d. {{ill|1011 AD|ja|1011th year }} ). * {{ill|Gennin|ja|元永}} 1st Year ([[Category:Lunar calendar translation marker]] {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1118 AD|ja|1118th year }} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}} ) – {{ill|Chiba Tsunetane|ja|千葉常胤}} , a military commander and protector (shugo) of {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国|lt=Shimōsa}} (d. {{ill|1201 AD|ja|1201st year }} ). * {{ill|Shōhō|ja|正保}} 1st Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1644 AD|ja|1644th year }} {{ill|June 28|ja|6月28日}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunashige|ja|徳川綱重}} , the first lord of the {{ill|Kōfu Domain|ja|甲府藩}} , the third son of {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} , and the father of {{ill|Tokugawa Ienobu|ja|徳川家宣}} (d. {{ill|1678 AD|ja|1678th year }} ). * {{ill|Kōka|ja|弘化}} 3rd Year (leap May) ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1846 AD|ja|1846th year }} {{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa Iemoto|ja|徳川家茂}} , the 14th shogun of the {{ill|Edo Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}} (d. {{ill|1866 AD|ja|1866th year }} ). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 4th Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1871 AD|ja|1871st year }} {{ill|July 11|ja|7月11日}} ) – {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉}} , a historian (d. {{ill|1939 AD|ja|1939th year }} ).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 13th Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1636 AD|ja|1636th year }} {{ill|June 27|ja|6月27日}} ) – {{ill|Date Masamune|ja|伊達政宗}} , a military commander and the first lord of the {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}} (born {{ill|1567 AD|ja|1567th year }} ).
== Commemorations == *
[[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category: tier 1]] [[ja:年表]]

(Dating based on Ikko-ikki inscription tiles is approximate; some sources propose Tenshō 8 ({{ill|1580 AD|ja|1580th year }} {{ill|July 5|ja|7月5日}} )). * {{ill|Kansei|ja|Kansei}} 2nd Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1790 AD|ja|1790th year }} {{ill|July 6|ja|7月6日}} ) – The Kansei Prohibition on Heterodox Learning was enacted, with {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信}} banning the teaching of subjects other than {{ill|Neo-Confucianism|ja|朱子学}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂}} .
== Births == * {{ill|Tenryaku|ja|天暦}} 4th Year ([[Category:Lunar calendar translation marker]] {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|950 AD|ja|950th year }} {{ill|June 12|ja|6月12日}} ) – {{ill|Emperor Reisō|ja|冷泉天皇}} , the 63rd {{ill|Emperor|ja|天皇}} (d. {{ill|1011 AD|ja|1011th year }} ). * {{ill|Gennin|ja|元永}} 1st Year ([[Category:Lunar calendar translation marker]] {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1118 AD|ja|1118th year }} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}} ) – {{ill|Chiba Tsunetane|ja|千葉常胤}} , a military commander and protector (shugo) of {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国|lt=Shimōsa}} (d. {{ill|1201 AD|ja|1201st year }} ). * {{ill|Shōhō|ja|正保}} 1st Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1644 AD|ja|1644th year }} {{ill|June 28|ja|6月28日}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunashige|ja|徳川綱重}} , the first lord of the {{ill|Kōfu Domain|ja|甲府藩}} , the third son of {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} , and the father of {{ill|Tokugawa Ienobu|ja|徳川家宣}} (d. {{ill|1678 AD|ja|1678th year }} ). * {{ill|Kōka|ja|弘化}} 3rd Year (leap May) ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1846 AD|ja|1846th year }} {{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa Iemoto|ja|徳川家茂}} , the 14th shogun of the {{ill|Edo Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}} (d. {{ill|1866 AD|ja|1866th year }} ). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 4th Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1871 AD|ja|1871st year }} {{ill|July 11|ja|7月11日}} ) – {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉}} , a historian (d. {{ill|1939 AD|ja|1939th year }} ).

[[sn:May 24 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 5월 24일]] [[cdo:5 nguŏk 24 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:5月24日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 5월 24일]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 24 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月廿四]] [[zh:五月廿四]] [[en:draft:May 24 (Lunisolar)]]
{{Lunisolar May}} '''May 24''' is the 24th day of month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May}} the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Butsumetsu|ja|仏滅}} .
== Events == * {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} 3rd Year ([[Category:Lunar calendar translation marker]] {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|699 AD|ja|699th year }} {{ill|June 26|ja|6月26日}} ) – The founder of Shugendō, {{ill|En no Ozunu|ja|役行者|lt=En no Ozunu|lt_ja=役小角}} , was exiled to {{ill|Izu Province|ja|Izu Province|lt=Izu|lt_ja=伊豆}} due to slander from his disciples. * {{ill|Bunji|ja|文治}} 1st Year ([[Category:Lunar calendar translation marker]] {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1185 AD|ja|1185th year }} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日}} ) – To appease {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} , {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|lt=Yoshitsune|lt_ja=義経}} , who was en route to Kamakura, was detained at {{ill|Koshigoe|ja|腰越}} , and thus entrusted a letter (the {{ill|Koshigoe Letter|ja|腰越状}} ) to {{ill|Ōe Hiromoto|ja|大江広元}} . * {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era}} 4th Year ([[Category:Lunar calendar translation marker]] {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1576 AD|ja|1576th year }} {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日}} ) – {{ill|Maeda Toshiie|ja|Maeda Toshiie}} executed approximately 1,000 captives of the {{ill|Echizen Ikko-ikki|ja|越前一向一揆}} , subjecting them to crucifixion and boiling. (Dating based on Ikko-ikki inscription tiles is approximate; some sources propose Tenshō 8 ({{ill|1580 AD|ja|1580th year }} {{ill|July 5|ja|7月5日}} )). * {{ill|Kansei|ja|Kansei}} 2nd Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1790 AD|ja|1790th year }} {{ill|July 6|ja|7月6日}} ) – The Kansei Prohibition on Heterodox Learning was enacted, with {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信}} banning the teaching of subjects other than {{ill|Neo-Confucianism|ja|朱子学}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂}} .
== Births == * {{ill|Tenryaku|ja|天暦}} 4th Year ([[Category:Lunar calendar translation marker]] {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|950 AD|ja|950th year }} {{ill|June 12|ja|6月12日}} ) – {{ill|Emperor Reisō|ja|冷泉天皇}} , the 63rd {{ill|Emperor|ja|天皇}} (d. {{ill|1011 AD|ja|1011th year }} ). * {{ill|Gennin|ja|元永}} 1st Year ([[Category:Lunar calendar translation marker]] {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1118 AD|ja|1118th year }} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}} ) – {{ill|Chiba Tsunetane|ja|千葉常胤}} , a military commander and protector (shugo) of {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国|lt=Shimōsa}} (d. {{ill|1201 AD|ja|1201st year }} ). * {{ill|Shōhō|ja|正保}} 1st Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1644 AD|ja|1644th year }} {{ill|June 28|ja|6月28日}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunashige|ja|徳川綱重}} , the first lord of the {{ill|Kōfu Domain|ja|甲府藩}} , the third son of {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} , and the father of {{ill|Tokugawa Ienobu|ja|徳川家宣}} (d. {{ill|1678 AD|ja|1678th year }} ). * {{ill|Kōka|ja|弘化}} 3rd Year (leap May) ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1846 AD|ja|1846th year }} {{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa Iemoto|ja|徳川家茂}} , the 14th shogun of the {{ill|Edo Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}} (d. {{ill|1866 AD|ja|1866th year }} ). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 4th Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1871 AD|ja|1871st year }} {{ill|July 11|ja|7月11日}} ) – {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉}} , a historian (d. {{ill|1939 AD|ja|1939th year }} ).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 13th Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1636 AD|ja|1636th year }} {{ill|June 27|ja|6月27日}} ) – {{ill|Date Masamune|ja|伊達政宗}} , a military commander and the first lord of the {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}} (born {{ill|1567 AD|ja|1567th year }} ).
== Commemorations == *
[[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category: tier 1]] [[ja:年表]]
== See Also ==
{{Lunar Calendar See Also|5|23|5|25|4|24|6|5|0524}}

[[sn:May 24 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 5월 24일]] [[cdo:5 nguŏk 24 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:5月24日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 5월 24일]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 24 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月廿四]] [[zh:五月廿四]] [[en:draft:May 24 (Lunisolar)]]
{{Lunisolar May}} '''May 24''' is the 24th day of month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May}} the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Butsumetsu|ja|仏滅}} .
== Events == * {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} 3rd Year ([[Category:Lunar calendar translation marker]] {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|699 AD|ja|699th year }} {{ill|June 26|ja|6月26日}} ) – The founder of Shugendō, {{ill|En no Ozunu|ja|役行者|lt=En no Ozunu|lt_ja=役小角}} , was exiled to {{ill|Izu Province|ja|Izu Province|lt=Izu|lt_ja=伊豆}} due to slander from his disciples. * {{ill|Bunji|ja|文治}} 1st Year ([[Category:Lunar calendar translation marker]] {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1185 AD|ja|1185th year }} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日}} ) – To appease {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} , {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|lt=Yoshitsune|lt_ja=義経}} , who was en route to Kamakura, was detained at {{ill|Koshigoe|ja|腰越}} , and thus entrusted a letter (the {{ill|Koshigoe Letter|ja|腰越状}} ) to {{ill|Ōe Hiromoto|ja|大江広元}} . * {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era}} 4th Year ([[Category:Lunar calendar translation marker]] {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1576 AD|ja|1576th year }} {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日}} ) – {{ill|Maeda Toshiie|ja|Maeda Toshiie}} executed approximately 1,000 captives of the {{ill|Echizen Ikko-ikki|ja|越前一向一揆}} , subjecting them to crucifixion and boiling. (Dating based on Ikko-ikki inscription tiles is approximate; some sources propose Tenshō 8 ({{ill|1580 AD|ja|1580th year }} {{ill|July 5|ja|7月5日}} )). * {{ill|Kansei|ja|Kansei}} 2nd Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1790 AD|ja|1790th year }} {{ill|July 6|ja|7月6日}} ) – The Kansei Prohibition on Heterodox Learning was enacted, with {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信}} banning the teaching of subjects other than {{ill|Neo-Confucianism|ja|朱子学}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂}} .
== Births == * {{ill|Tenryaku|ja|天暦}} 4th Year ([[Category:Lunar calendar translation marker]] {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|950 AD|ja|950th year }} {{ill|June 12|ja|6月12日}} ) – {{ill|Emperor Reisō|ja|冷泉天皇}} , the 63rd {{ill|Emperor|ja|天皇}} (d. {{ill|1011 AD|ja|1011th year }} ). * {{ill|Gennin|ja|元永}} 1st Year ([[Category:Lunar calendar translation marker]] {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1118 AD|ja|1118th year }} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}} ) – {{ill|Chiba Tsunetane|ja|千葉常胤}} , a military commander and protector (shugo) of {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国|lt=Shimōsa}} (d. {{ill|1201 AD|ja|1201st year }} ). * {{ill|Shōhō|ja|正保}} 1st Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1644 AD|ja|1644th year }} {{ill|June 28|ja|6月28日}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunashige|ja|徳川綱重}} , the first lord of the {{ill|Kōfu Domain|ja|甲府藩}} , the third son of {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} , and the father of {{ill|Tokugawa Ienobu|ja|徳川家宣}} (d. {{ill|1678 AD|ja|1678th year }} ). * {{ill|Kōka|ja|弘化}} 3rd Year (leap May) ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1846 AD|ja|1846th year }} {{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa Iemoto|ja|徳川家茂}} , the 14th shogun of the {{ill|Edo Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}} (d. {{ill|1866 AD|ja|1866th year }} ). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 4th Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1871 AD|ja|1871st year }} {{ill|July 11|ja|7月11日}} ) – {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉}} , a historian (d. {{ill|1939 AD|ja|1939th year }} ).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 13th Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1636 AD|ja|1636th year }} {{ill|June 27|ja|6月27日}} ) – {{ill|Date Masamune|ja|伊達政宗}} , a military commander and the first lord of the {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}} (born {{ill|1567 AD|ja|1567th year }} ).
== Commemorations == *
[[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category: tier 5]][[ja:年表]]

== Events == * {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} 3rd Year ([[Category:Lunar calendar translation marker]] {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|699 AD|ja|699th year }} {{ill|June 26|ja|6月26日}} ) – The founder of Shugendō, {{ill|En no Ozunu|ja|役行者|lt=En no Ozunu|lt_ja=役小角}} , was exiled to {{ill|Izu Province|ja|Izu Province|lt=Izu|lt_ja=伊豆}} due to slander from his disciples. * {{ill|Bunji|ja|文治}} 1st Year ([[Category:Lunar calendar translation marker]] {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1185 AD|ja|1185th year }} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日}} ) – To appease {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} , {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|lt=Yoshitsune|lt_ja=義経}} , who was en route to Kamakura, was detained at {{ill|Koshigoe|ja|腰越}} , and thus entrusted a letter (the {{ill|Koshigoe Letter|ja|腰越状}} ) to {{ill|Ōe Hiromoto|ja|大江広元}} . * {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era}} 4th Year ([[Category:Lunar calendar translation marker]] {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1576 AD|ja|1576th year }} {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日}} ) – {{ill|Maeda Toshiie|ja|Maeda Toshiie}} executed approximately 1,000 captives of the {{ill|Echizen Ikko-ikki|ja|越前一向一揆}} , subjecting them to crucifixion and boiling. (Dating based on Ikko-ikki inscription tiles is approximate; some sources propose Tenshō 8 ({{ill|1580 AD|ja|1580th year }} {{ill|July 5|ja|7月5日}} )). * {{ill|Kansei|ja|Kansei}} 2nd Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1790 AD|ja|1790th year }} {{ill|July 6|ja|7月6日}} ) – The Kansei Prohibition on Heterodox Learning was enacted, with {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信}} banning the teaching of subjects other than {{ill|Neo-Confucianism|ja|朱子学}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂}} .
== Births == * {{ill|Tenryaku|ja|天暦}} 4th Year ([[Category:Lunar calendar translation marker]] {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|950 AD|ja|950th year }} {{ill|June 12|ja|6月12日}} ) – {{ill|Emperor Reisō|ja|冷泉天皇}} , the 63rd {{ill|Emperor|ja|天皇}} (d. {{ill|1011 AD|ja|1011th year }} ). * {{ill|Gennin|ja|元永}} 1st Year ([[Category:Lunar calendar translation marker]] {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1118 AD|ja|1118th year }} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}} ) – {{ill|Chiba Tsunetane|ja|千葉常胤}} , a military commander and protector (shugo) of {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国|lt=Shimōsa}} (d. {{ill|1201 AD|ja|1201st year }} ). * {{ill|Shōhō|ja|正保}} 1st Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1644 AD|ja|1644th year }} {{ill|June 28|ja|6月28日}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunashige|ja|徳川綱重}} , the first lord of the {{ill|Kōfu Domain|ja|甲府藩}} , the third son of {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} , and the father of {{ill|Tokugawa Ienobu|ja|徳川家宣}} (d. {{ill|1678 AD|ja|1678th year }} ). * {{ill|Kōka|ja|弘化}} 3rd Year (leap May) ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1846 AD|ja|1846th year }} {{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa Iemoto|ja|徳川家茂}} , the 14th shogun of the {{ill|Edo Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}} (d. {{ill|1866 AD|ja|1866th year }} ). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 4th Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1871 AD|ja|1871st year }} {{ill|July 11|ja|7月11日}} ) – {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉}} , a historian (d. {{ill|1939 AD|ja|1939th year }} ).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 13th Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1636 AD|ja|1636th year }} {{ill|June 27|ja|6月27日}} ) – {{ill|Date Masamune|ja|伊達政宗}} , a military commander and the first lord of the {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}} (born {{ill|1567 AD|ja|1567th year }} ).
== Commemorations == *
[[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category: tier 1]] [[ja:年表]]
== See Also ==

[[sn:May 24 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 5월 24일]] [[cdo:5 nguŏk 24 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:5月24日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 5월 24일]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 24 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月廿四]] [[zh:五月廿四]] [[en:draft:May 24 (Lunisolar)]]
{{Lunisolar May}} '''May 24''' is the 24th day of month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May}} the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Butsumetsu|ja|仏滅}} .
== Events == * {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} 3rd Year ([[Category:Lunar calendar translation marker]] {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|699 AD|ja|699th year }} {{ill|June 26|ja|6月26日}} ) – The founder of Shugendō, {{ill|En no Ozunu|ja|役行者|lt=En no Ozunu|lt_ja=役小角}} , was exiled to {{ill|Izu Province|ja|Izu Province|lt=Izu|lt_ja=伊豆}} due to slander from his disciples. * {{ill|Bunji|ja|文治}} 1st Year ([[Category:Lunar calendar translation marker]] {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1185 AD|ja|1185th year }} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日}} ) – To appease {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} , {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|lt=Yoshitsune|lt_ja=義経}} , who was en route to Kamakura, was detained at {{ill|Koshigoe|ja|腰越}} , and thus entrusted a letter (the {{ill|Koshigoe Letter|ja|腰越状}} ) to {{ill|Ōe Hiromoto|ja|大江広元}} . * {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era}} 4th Year ([[Category:Lunar calendar translation marker]] {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1576 AD|ja|1576th year }} {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日}} ) – {{ill|Maeda Toshiie|ja|Maeda Toshiie}} executed approximately 1,000 captives of the {{ill|Echizen Ikko-ikki|ja|越前一向一揆}} , subjecting them to crucifixion and boiling. (Dating based on Ikko-ikki inscription tiles is approximate; some sources propose Tenshō 8 ({{ill|1580 AD|ja|1580th year }} {{ill|July 5|ja|7月5日}} )). * {{ill|Kansei|ja|Kansei}} 2nd Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1790 AD|ja|1790th year }} {{ill|July 6|ja|7月6日}} ) – The Kansei Prohibition on Heterodox Learning was enacted, with {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信}} banning the teaching of subjects other than {{ill|Neo-Confucianism|ja|朱子学}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂}} .
== Births == * {{ill|Tenryaku|ja|天暦}} 4th Year ([[Category:Lunar calendar translation marker]] {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|950 AD|ja|950th year }} {{ill|June 12|ja|6月12日}} ) – {{ill|Emperor Reisō|ja|冷泉天皇}} , the 63rd {{ill|Emperor|ja|天皇}} (d. {{ill|1011 AD|ja|1011th year }} ). * {{ill|Gennin|ja|元永}} 1st Year ([[Category:Lunar calendar translation marker]] {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1118 AD|ja|1118th year }} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}} ) – {{ill|Chiba Tsunetane|ja|千葉常胤}} , a military commander and protector (shugo) of {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国|lt=Shimōsa}} (d. {{ill|1201 AD|ja|1201st year }} ). * {{ill|Shōhō|ja|正保}} 1st Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1644 AD|ja|1644th year }} {{ill|June 28|ja|6月28日}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunashige|ja|徳川綱重}} , the first lord of the {{ill|Kōfu Domain|ja|甲府藩}} , the third son of {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} , and the father of {{ill|Tokugawa Ienobu|ja|徳川家宣}} (d. {{ill|1678 AD|ja|1678th year }} ). * {{ill|Kōka|ja|弘化}} 3rd Year (leap May) ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1846 AD|ja|1846th year }} {{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa Iemoto|ja|徳川家茂}} , the 14th shogun of the {{ill|Edo Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}} (d. {{ill|1866 AD|ja|1866th year }} ). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 4th Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1871 AD|ja|1871st year }} {{ill|July 11|ja|7月11日}} ) – {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉}} , a historian (d. {{ill|1939 AD|ja|1939th year }} ).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 13th Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1636 AD|ja|1636th year }} {{ill|June 27|ja|6月27日}} ) – {{ill|Date Masamune|ja|伊達政宗}} , a military commander and the first lord of the {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}} (born {{ill|1567 AD|ja|1567th year }} ).

revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
[[cdo:5 nguŏk 24 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 24 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月廿四]]
{{Lunisolar May}} '''May 24''' is the 24th day of month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May}} the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Butsumetsu|ja|仏滅}} .
== Events == * {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} 3rd Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|699 AD|ja|699th year }} {{ill|June 26|ja|6月26日}} ) – The founder of Shugendō, {{ill|En no Ozunu|ja|役行者|lt=En no Ozunu|lt_ja=役小角}} , was exiled to {{ill|Izu Province|ja|Izu Province|lt=Izu|lt_ja=伊豆}} due to slander from his disciples. * {{ill|Bunji|ja|文治}} 1st Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1185 AD|ja|1185th year }} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日}} ) – To appease {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} , {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|lt=Yoshitsune|lt_ja=義経}} , who was en route to Kamakura, was detained at {{ill|Koshigoe|ja|腰越}} , and thus entrusted a letter (the {{ill|Koshigoe Letter|ja|腰越状}} ) to {{ill|Ōe Hiromoto|ja|大江広元}} . * {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era}} 4th Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1576 AD|ja|1576th year }} {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日}} ) – {{ill|Maeda Toshiie|ja|Maeda Toshiie}} executed approximately 1,000 captives of the {{ill|Echizen Ikko-ikki|ja|越前一向一揆}} , subjecting them to crucifixion and boiling. (Dating based on Ikko-ikki inscription tiles is approximate; some sources propose Tenshō 8 ({{ill|1580 AD|ja|1580th year }} {{ill|July 5|ja|7月5日}} )). * {{ill|Kansei|ja|Kansei}} 2nd Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1790 AD|ja|1790th year }} {{ill|July 6|ja|7月6日}} ) – The Kansei Prohibition on Heterodox Learning was enacted, with {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信}} banning the teaching of subjects other than {{ill|Neo-Confucianism|ja|朱子学}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂}} .
== Births == * {{ill|Tenryaku|ja|天暦}} 4th Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|950 AD|ja|950th year }} {{ill|June 12|ja|6月12日}} ) – {{ill|Emperor Reisō|ja|冷泉天皇}} , the 63rd {{ill|Emperor|ja|天皇}} (d. {{ill|1011 AD|ja|1011th year }} ). * {{ill|Gennin|ja|元永}} 1st Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1118 AD|ja|1118th year }} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}} ) – {{ill|Chiba Tsunetane|ja|千葉常胤}} , a military commander and protector (shugo) of {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国|lt=Shimōsa}} (d. {{ill|1201 AD|ja|1201st year }} ). * {{ill|Shōhō|ja|正保}} 1st Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1644 AD|ja|1644th year }} {{ill|June 28|ja|6月28日}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunashige|ja|徳川綱重}} , the first lord of the {{ill|Kōfu Domain|ja|甲府藩}} , the third son of {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} , and the father of {{ill|Tokugawa Ienobu|ja|徳川家宣}} (d. {{ill|1678 AD|ja|1678th year }} ). * {{ill|Kōka|ja|弘化}} 3rd Year (leap May) ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1846 AD|ja|1846th year }} {{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa Iemoto|ja|徳川家茂}} , the 14th shogun of the {{ill|Edo Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}} (d. {{ill|1866 AD|ja|1866th year }} ). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 4th Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1871 AD|ja|1871st year }} {{ill|July 11|ja|7月11日}} ) – {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉}} , a historian (d. {{ill|1939 AD|ja|1939th year }} ).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 13th Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1636 AD|ja|1636th year }} {{ill|June 27|ja|6月27日}} ) – {{ill|Date Masamune|ja|伊達政宗}} , a military commander and the first lord of the {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}} (born {{ill|1567 AD|ja|1567th year }} ).
== Commemorations == *
== See Also ==
{{Lunar Calendar See Also|5|23|5|25|4|24|6|5|0524}}
[[cdo:5 nguŏk 24 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 24 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月廿四]]

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Lunar calendar translation marker]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[cdo:5 nguŏk 24 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 24 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月廿四]]
{{Lunisolar May}} '''May 24''' is the 24th day of month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May|12=draft}} the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=draft}} day is {{ill|Butsumetsu|ja|仏滅|12=draft}} .
== Events == * {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|12=draft}} 3rd Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft}} {{ill|699 AD|ja|699th year|12=draft}} {{ill|June 26|ja|6月26日|12=draft}} ) – The founder of Shugendō, {{ill|En no Ozunu|ja|役行者|lt=En no Ozunu|lt_ja=役小角|12=draft}} , was exiled to {{ill|Izu Province|ja|Izu Province|lt=Izu|lt_ja=伊豆|12=draft}} due to slander from his disciples. * {{ill|Bunji|ja|文治|12=draft}} 1st Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft}} {{ill|1185 AD|ja|1185th year|12=draft}} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日|12=draft}} ) – To appease {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft}} , {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|lt=Yoshitsune|lt_ja=義経|12=draft}} , who was en route to Kamakura, was detained at {{ill|Koshigoe|ja|腰越|12=draft}} , and thus entrusted a letter (the {{ill|Koshigoe Letter|ja|腰越状|12=draft}} ) to {{ill|Ōe Hiromoto|ja|大江広元|12=draft}} . * {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|12=draft}} 4th Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft}} {{ill|1576 AD|ja|1576th year|12=draft}} {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Maeda Toshiie|ja|Maeda Toshiie|12=draft}} executed approximately 1,000 captives of the {{ill|Echizen Ikko-ikki|ja|越前一向一揆|12=draft}} , subjecting them to crucifixion and boiling. (Dating based on Ikko-ikki inscription tiles is approximate; some sources propose Tenshō 8 ({{ill|1580 AD|ja|1580th year|12=draft}} {{ill|July 5|ja|7月5日|12=draft}} )). * {{ill|Kansei|ja|Kansei|12=draft}} 2nd Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}} {{ill|1790 AD|ja|1790th year|12=draft}} {{ill|July 6|ja|7月6日|12=draft}} ) – The Kansei Prohibition on Heterodox Learning was enacted, with {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信|12=draft}} banning the teaching of subjects other than {{ill|Neo-Confucianism|ja|朱子学|12=draft}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂|12=draft}} .
== Births == * {{ill|Tenryaku|ja|天暦|12=draft}} 4th Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft}} {{ill|950 AD|ja|950th year|12=draft}} {{ill|June 12|ja|6月12日|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Emperor Reisō|ja|冷泉天皇|12=draft}} , the 63rd {{ill|Emperor|ja|天皇|12=draft}} (d. {{ill|1011 AD|ja|1011th year|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Gennin|ja|元永|12=draft}} 1st Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft}} {{ill|1118 AD|ja|1118th year|12=draft}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Chiba Tsunetane|ja|千葉常胤|12=draft}} , a military commander and protector (shugo) of {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国|lt=Shimōsa|12=draft}} (d. {{ill|1201 AD|ja|1201st year|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Shōhō|ja|正保|12=draft}} 1st Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}} {{ill|1644 AD|ja|1644th year|12=draft}} {{ill|June 28|ja|6月28日|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunashige|ja|徳川綱重|12=draft}} , the first lord of the {{ill|Kōfu Domain|ja|甲府藩|12=draft}} , the third son of {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光|12=draft}} , and the father of {{ill|Tokugawa Ienobu|ja|徳川家宣|12=draft}} (d. {{ill|1678 AD|ja|1678th year|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Kōka|ja|弘化|12=draft}} 3rd Year (leap May) ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}} {{ill|1846 AD|ja|1846th year|12=draft}} {{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa Iemoto|ja|徳川家茂|12=draft}} , the 14th shogun of the {{ill|Edo Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|12=draft}} (d. {{ill|1866 AD|ja|1866th year|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 4th Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}} {{ill|1871 AD|ja|1871st year|12=draft}} {{ill|July 11|ja|7月11日|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉|12=draft}} , a historian (d. {{ill|1939 AD|ja|1939th year|12=draft}} ).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|12=draft}} 13th Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}} {{ill|1636 AD|ja|1636th year|12=draft}} {{ill|June 27|ja|6月27日|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Date Masamune|ja|伊達政宗|12=draft}} , a military commander and the first lord of the {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩|12=draft}} (born {{ill|1567 AD|ja|1567th year|12=draft}} ).
== Commemorations == *
== See Also ==
{{Lunar Calendar See Also|5|23|5|25|4|24|6|5|0524}}
[[cdo:5 nguŏk 24 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 24 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月廿四]]

{{Lunisolar May}} '''May 24''' is the 24th day of month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May|12=draft}} the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=draft}} day is {{ill|Butsumetsu|ja|仏滅|12=draft}} .
== Events == * {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|12=draft}} 3rd Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft}} {{ill|699 AD|ja|699th year|12=draft}} {{ill|June 26|ja|6月26日|12=draft}} ) – The founder of Shugendō, {{ill|En no Ozunu|ja|役行者|lt=En no Ozunu|lt_ja=役小角|12=draft}} , was exiled to {{ill|Izu Province|ja|Izu Province|lt=Izu|lt_ja=伊豆|12=draft}} due to slander from his disciples. * {{ill|Bunji|ja|文治|12=draft}} 1st Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft}} {{ill|1185 AD|ja|1185th year|12=draft}} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日|12=draft}} ) – To appease {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft}} , {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|lt=Yoshitsune|lt_ja=義経|12=draft}} , who was en route to Kamakura, was detained at {{ill|Koshigoe|ja|腰越|12=draft}} , and thus entrusted a letter (the {{ill|Koshigoe Letter|ja|腰越状|12=draft}} ) to {{ill|Ōe Hiromoto|ja|大江広元|12=draft}} . * {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|12=draft}} 4th Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft}} {{ill|1576 AD|ja|1576th year|12=draft}} {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Maeda Toshiie|ja|Maeda Toshiie|12=draft}} executed approximately 1,000 captives of the {{ill|Echizen Ikko-ikki|ja|越前一向一揆|12=draft}} , subjecting them to crucifixion and boiling. (Dating based on Ikko-ikki inscription tiles is approximate; some sources propose Tenshō 8 ({{ill|1580 AD|ja|1580th year|12=draft}} {{ill|July 5|ja|7月5日|12=draft}} )). * {{ill|Kansei|ja|Kansei|12=draft}} 2nd Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}} {{ill|1790 AD|ja|1790th year|12=draft}} {{ill|July 6|ja|7月6日|12=draft}} ) – The Kansei Prohibition on Heterodox Learning was enacted, with {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信|12=draft}} banning the teaching of subjects other than {{ill|Neo-Confucianism|ja|朱子学|12=draft}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂|12=draft}} .
== Births == * {{ill|Tenryaku|ja|天暦|12=draft}} 4th Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft}} {{ill|950 AD|ja|950th year|12=draft}} {{ill|June 12|ja|6月12日|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Emperor Reisō|ja|冷泉天皇|12=draft}} , the 63rd {{ill|Emperor|ja|天皇|12=draft}} (d. {{ill|1011 AD|ja|1011th year|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Gennin|ja|元永|12=draft}} 1st Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft}} {{ill|1118 AD|ja|1118th year|12=draft}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Chiba Tsunetane|ja|千葉常胤|12=draft}} , a military commander and protector (shugo) of {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国|lt=Shimōsa|12=draft}} (d. {{ill|1201 AD|ja|1201st year|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Shōhō|ja|正保|12=draft}} 1st Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}} {{ill|1644 AD|ja|1644th year|12=draft}} {{ill|June 28|ja|6月28日|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunashige|ja|徳川綱重|12=draft}} , the first lord of the {{ill|Kōfu Domain|ja|甲府藩|12=draft}} , the third son of {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光|12=draft}} , and the father of {{ill|Tokugawa Ienobu|ja|徳川家宣|12=draft}} (d. {{ill|1678 AD|ja|1678th year|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Kōka|ja|弘化|12=draft}} 3rd Year (leap May) ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}} {{ill|1846 AD|ja|1846th year|12=draft}} {{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa Iemoto|ja|徳川家茂|12=draft}} , the 14th shogun of the {{ill|Edo Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|12=draft}} (d. {{ill|1866 AD|ja|1866th year|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 4th Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}} {{ill|1871 AD|ja|1871st year|12=draft}} {{ill|July 11|ja|7月11日|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉|12=draft}} , a historian (d. {{ill|1939 AD|ja|1939th year|12=draft}} ).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|12=draft}} 13th Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}} {{ill|1636 AD|ja|1636th year|12=draft}} {{ill|June 27|ja|6月27日|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Date Masamune|ja|伊達政宗|12=draft}} , a military commander and the first lord of the {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩|12=draft}} (born {{ill|1567 AD|ja|1567th year|12=draft}} ).
== Commemorations == *
== See Also ==
{{Lunar Calendar See Also|5|23|5|25|4|24|6|5|0524}}
[[cdo:5 nguŏk 24 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 24 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月廿四]]

{{Lunisolar May}} '''May 24''' is the 24th day of month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May|12=draft|12=draft}} the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft|12=draft}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=draft|12=draft}} day is {{ill|Butsumetsu|ja|仏滅|12=draft|12=draft}} .
== Events == * {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} 3rd Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|699 AD|ja|699th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 26|ja|6月26日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The founder of Shugendō, {{ill|En no Ozunu|ja|役行者|lt=En no Ozunu|lt_ja=役小角|12=draft|12=draft}} , was exiled to {{ill|Izu Province|ja|Izu Province|lt=Izu|lt_ja=伊豆|12=draft|12=draft}} due to slander from his disciples. * {{ill|Bunji|ja|文治|12=draft|12=draft}} 1st Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1185 AD|ja|1185th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – To appease {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft|12=draft}} , {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|lt=Yoshitsune|lt_ja=義経|12=draft|12=draft}} , who was en route to Kamakura, was detained at {{ill|Koshigoe|ja|腰越|12=draft|12=draft}} , and thus entrusted a letter (the {{ill|Koshigoe Letter|ja|腰越状|12=draft|12=draft}} ) to {{ill|Ōe Hiromoto|ja|大江広元|12=draft|12=draft}} . * {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|12=draft|12=draft}} 4th Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1576 AD|ja|1576th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Maeda Toshiie|ja|Maeda Toshiie|12=draft|12=draft}} executed approximately 1,000 captives of the {{ill|Echizen Ikko-ikki|ja|越前一向一揆|12=draft|12=draft}} , subjecting them to crucifixion and boiling. (Dating based on Ikko-ikki inscription tiles is approximate; some sources propose Tenshō 8 ({{ill|1580 AD|ja|1580th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|July 5|ja|7月5日|12=draft|12=draft}} )). * {{ill|Kansei|ja|Kansei|12=draft|12=draft}} 2nd Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1790 AD|ja|1790th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|July 6|ja|7月6日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The Kansei Prohibition on Heterodox Learning was enacted, with {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信|12=draft|12=draft}} banning the teaching of subjects other than {{ill|Neo-Confucianism|ja|朱子学|12=draft|12=draft}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂|12=draft|12=draft}} .
== Births == * {{ill|Tenryaku|ja|天暦|12=draft|12=draft}} 4th Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|950 AD|ja|950th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 12|ja|6月12日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Emperor Reisō|ja|冷泉天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} , the 63rd {{ill|Emperor|ja|天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (d. {{ill|1011 AD|ja|1011th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Gennin|ja|元永|12=draft|12=draft}} 1st Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1118 AD|ja|1118th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Chiba Tsunetane|ja|千葉常胤|12=draft|12=draft}} , a military commander and protector (shugo) of {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国|lt=Shimōsa|12=draft|12=draft}} (d. {{ill|1201 AD|ja|1201st year|12=draft|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Shōhō|ja|正保|12=draft|12=draft}} 1st Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1644 AD|ja|1644th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 28|ja|6月28日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunashige|ja|徳川綱重|12=draft|12=draft}} , the first lord of the {{ill|Kōfu Domain|ja|甲府藩|12=draft|12=draft}} , the third son of {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光|12=draft|12=draft}} , and the father of {{ill|Tokugawa Ienobu|ja|徳川家宣|12=draft|12=draft}} (d. {{ill|1678 AD|ja|1678th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Kōka|ja|弘化|12=draft|12=draft}} 3rd Year (leap May) ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1846 AD|ja|1846th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa Iemoto|ja|徳川家茂|12=draft|12=draft}} , the 14th shogun of the {{ill|Edo Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|12=draft|12=draft}} (d. {{ill|1866 AD|ja|1866th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} 4th Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1871 AD|ja|1871st year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|July 11|ja|7月11日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉|12=draft|12=draft}} , a historian (d. {{ill|1939 AD|ja|1939th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 13th Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1636 AD|ja|1636th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 27|ja|6月27日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Date Masamune|ja|伊達政宗|12=draft|12=draft}} , a military commander and the first lord of the {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩|12=draft|12=draft}} (born {{ill|1567 AD|ja|1567th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== Commemorations == *

{{Lunisolar May}} '''May 24''' is the 24th day of month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May|12=draft|12=draft}} the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft|12=draft}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=draft|12=draft}} day is {{ill|Butsumetsu|ja|仏滅|12=draft|12=draft}} .
== Events == * {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} 3rd Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|699 AD|ja|699th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 26|ja|6月26日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The founder of Shugendō, {{ill|En no Ozunu|ja|役行者|lt=En no Ozunu|lt_ja=役小角|12=draft|12=draft}} , was exiled to {{ill|Izu Province|ja|Izu Province|lt=Izu|lt_ja=伊豆|12=draft|12=draft}} due to slander from his disciples. * {{ill|Bunji|ja|文治|12=draft|12=draft}} 1st Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1185 AD|ja|1185th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – To appease {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft|12=draft}} , {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|lt=Yoshitsune|lt_ja=義経|12=draft|12=draft}} , who was en route to Kamakura, was detained at {{ill|Koshigoe|ja|腰越|12=draft|12=draft}} , and thus entrusted a letter (the {{ill|Koshigoe Letter|ja|腰越状|12=draft|12=draft}} ) to {{ill|Ōe Hiromoto|ja|大江広元|12=draft|12=draft}} . * {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|12=draft|12=draft}} 4th Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1576 AD|ja|1576th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Maeda Toshiie|ja|Maeda Toshiie|12=draft|12=draft}} executed approximately 1,000 captives of the {{ill|Echizen Ikko-ikki|ja|越前一向一揆|12=draft|12=draft}} , subjecting them to crucifixion and boiling. (Dating based on Ikko-ikki inscription tiles is approximate; some sources propose Tenshō 8 ({{ill|1580 AD|ja|1580th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|July 5|ja|7月5日|12=draft|12=draft}} )). * {{ill|Kansei|ja|Kansei|12=draft|12=draft}} 2nd Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1790 AD|ja|1790th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|July 6|ja|7月6日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The Kansei Prohibition on Heterodox Learning was enacted, with {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信|12=draft|12=draft}} banning the teaching of subjects other than {{ill|Neo-Confucianism|ja|朱子学|12=draft|12=draft}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂|12=draft|12=draft}} .
== Births == * {{ill|Tenryaku|ja|天暦|12=draft|12=draft}} 4th Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|950 AD|ja|950th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 12|ja|6月12日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Emperor Reisō|ja|冷泉天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} , the 63rd {{ill|Emperor|ja|天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (d. {{ill|1011 AD|ja|1011th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Gennin|ja|元永|12=draft|12=draft}} 1st Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1118 AD|ja|1118th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Chiba Tsunetane|ja|千葉常胤|12=draft|12=draft}} , a military commander and protector (shugo) of {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国|lt=Shimōsa|12=draft|12=draft}} (d. {{ill|1201 AD|ja|1201st year|12=draft|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Shōhō|ja|正保|12=draft|12=draft}} 1st Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1644 AD|ja|1644th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 28|ja|6月28日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunashige|ja|徳川綱重|12=draft|12=draft}} , the first lord of the {{ill|Kōfu Domain|ja|甲府藩|12=draft|12=draft}} , the third son of {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光|12=draft|12=draft}} , and the father of {{ill|Tokugawa Ienobu|ja|徳川家宣|12=draft|12=draft}} (d. {{ill|1678 AD|ja|1678th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Kōka|ja|弘化|12=draft|12=draft}} 3rd Year (leap May) ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1846 AD|ja|1846th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa Iemoto|ja|徳川家茂|12=draft|12=draft}} , the 14th shogun of the {{ill|Edo Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|12=draft|12=draft}} (d. {{ill|1866 AD|ja|1866th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} 4th Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1871 AD|ja|1871st year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|July 11|ja|7月11日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉|12=draft|12=draft}} , a historian (d. {{ill|1939 AD|ja|1939th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 13th Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1636 AD|ja|1636th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 27|ja|6月27日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Date Masamune|ja|伊達政宗|12=draft|12=draft}} , a military commander and the first lord of the {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩|12=draft|12=draft}} (born {{ill|1567 AD|ja|1567th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ).

Iemoto|ja|徳川家茂|12=draft|12=draft}} , the 14th shogun of the {{ill|Edo Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|12=draft|12=draft}} (d. {{ill|1866 AD|ja|1866th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} 4th Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1871 AD|ja|1871st year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|July 11|ja|7月11日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉|12=draft|12=draft}} , a historian (d. {{ill|1939 AD|ja|1939th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ).

revision.text:
[[cdo:5 nguŏk 24 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 24 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月廿四]]
{{Lunisolar May}} '''May 24''' is the 24th day of month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May|12=draft|12=draft}} the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft|12=draft}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=draft|12=draft}} day is {{ill|Butsumetsu|ja|仏滅|12=draft|12=draft}} .
== Events == * {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} 3rd Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|699 AD|ja|699th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 26|ja|6月26日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The founder of Shugendō, {{ill|En no Ozunu|ja|役行者|lt=En no Ozunu|lt_ja=役小角|12=draft|12=draft}} , was exiled to {{ill|Izu Province|ja|Izu Province|lt=Izu|lt_ja=伊豆|12=draft|12=draft}} due to slander from his disciples. * {{ill|Bunji|ja|文治|12=draft|12=draft}} 1st Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1185 AD|ja|1185th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – To appease {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft|12=draft}} , {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|lt=Yoshitsune|lt_ja=義経|12=draft|12=draft}} , who was en route to Kamakura, was detained at {{ill|Koshigoe|ja|腰越|12=draft|12=draft}} , and thus entrusted a letter (the {{ill|Koshigoe Letter|ja|腰越状|12=draft|12=draft}} ) to {{ill|Ōe Hiromoto|ja|大江広元|12=draft|12=draft}} . * {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|12=draft|12=draft}} 4th Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1576 AD|ja|1576th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Maeda Toshiie|ja|Maeda Toshiie|12=draft|12=draft}} executed approximately 1,000 captives of the {{ill|Echizen Ikko-ikki|ja|越前一向一揆|12=draft|12=draft}} , subjecting them to crucifixion and boiling. (Dating based on Ikko-ikki inscription tiles is approximate; some sources propose Tenshō 8 ({{ill|1580 AD|ja|1580th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|July 5|ja|7月5日|12=draft|12=draft}} )). * {{ill|Kansei|ja|Kansei|12=draft|12=draft}} 2nd Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1790 AD|ja|1790th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|July 6|ja|7月6日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The Kansei Prohibition on Heterodox Learning was enacted, with {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信|12=draft|12=draft}} banning the teaching of subjects other than {{ill|Neo-Confucianism|ja|朱子学|12=draft|12=draft}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂|12=draft|12=draft}} .
== Births == * {{ill|Tenryaku|ja|天暦|12=draft|12=draft}} 4th Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|950 AD|ja|950th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 12|ja|6月12日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Emperor Reisō|ja|冷泉天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} , the 63rd {{ill|Emperor|ja|天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (d. {{ill|1011 AD|ja|1011th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Gennin|ja|元永|12=draft|12=draft}} 1st Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1118 AD|ja|1118th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Chiba Tsunetane|ja|千葉常胤|12=draft|12=draft}} , a military commander and protector (shugo) of {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国|lt=Shimōsa|12=draft|12=draft}} (d. {{ill|1201 AD|ja|1201st year|12=draft|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Shōhō|ja|正保|12=draft|12=draft}} 1st Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1644 AD|ja|1644th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 28|ja|6月28日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunashige|ja|徳川綱重|12=draft|12=draft}} , the first lord of the {{ill|Kōfu Domain|ja|甲府藩|12=draft|12=draft}} , the third son of {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光|12=draft|12=draft}} , and the father of {{ill|Tokugawa Ienobu|ja|徳川家宣|12=draft|12=draft}} (d. {{ill|1678 AD|ja|1678th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Kōka|ja|弘化|12=draft|12=draft}} 3rd Year (leap May) ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1846 AD|ja|1846th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa Iemoto|ja|徳川家茂|12=draft|12=draft}} , the 14th shogun of the {{ill|Edo Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|12=draft|12=draft}} (d. {{ill|1866 AD|ja|1866th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} 4th Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1871 AD|ja|1871st year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|July 11|ja|7月11日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉|12=draft|12=draft}} , a historian (d. {{ill|1939 AD|ja|1939th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 13th Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1636 AD|ja|1636th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 27|ja|6月27日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Date Masamune|ja|伊達政宗|12=draft|12=draft}} , a military commander and the first lord of the {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩|12=draft|12=draft}} (born {{ill|1567 AD|ja|1567th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== Commemorations == *
== See Also ==
{{Lunar Calendar See Also|5|23|5|25|4|24|6|5|0524}}

{{Lunisolar May}} '''May 24''' is the 24th day of month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May|12=draft|12=draft}} the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft|12=draft}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=draft|12=draft}} day is {{ill|Butsumetsu|ja|仏滅|12=draft|12=draft}} .
== Events == * {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} 3rd Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|699 AD|ja|699th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 26|ja|6月26日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The founder of Shugendō, {{ill|En no Ozunu|ja|役行者|lt=En no Ozunu|lt_ja=役小角|12=draft|12=draft}} , was exiled to {{ill|Izu Province|ja|Izu Province|lt=Izu|lt_ja=伊豆|12=draft|12=draft}} due to slander from his disciples. * {{ill|Bunji|ja|文治|12=draft|12=draft}} 1st Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1185 AD|ja|1185th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – To appease {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft|12=draft}} , {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|lt=Yoshitsune|lt_ja=義経|12=draft|12=draft}} , who was en route to Kamakura, was detained at {{ill|Koshigoe|ja|腰越|12=draft|12=draft}} , and thus entrusted a letter (the {{ill|Koshigoe Letter|ja|腰越状|12=draft|12=draft}} ) to {{ill|Ōe Hiromoto|ja|大江広元|12=draft|12=draft}} . * {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|12=draft|12=draft}} 4th Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1576 AD|ja|1576th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Maeda Toshiie|ja|Maeda Toshiie|12=draft|12=draft}} executed approximately 1,000 captives of the {{ill|Echizen Ikko-ikki|ja|越前一向一揆|12=draft|12=draft}} , subjecting them to crucifixion and boiling. (Dating based on Ikko-ikki inscription tiles is approximate; some sources propose Tenshō 8 ({{ill|1580 AD|ja|1580th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|July 5|ja|7月5日|12=draft|12=draft}} )). * {{ill|Kansei|ja|Kansei|12=draft|12=draft}} 2nd Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1790 AD|ja|1790th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|July 6|ja|7月6日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The Kansei Prohibition on Heterodox Learning was enacted, with {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信|12=draft|12=draft}} banning the teaching of subjects other than {{ill|Neo-Confucianism|ja|朱子学|12=draft|12=draft}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂|12=draft|12=draft}} .
== Births == * {{ill|Tenryaku|ja|天暦|12=draft|12=draft}} 4th Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|950 AD|ja|950th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 3rd Year (leap May) ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1846 AD|ja|1846th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa (Dating based on Ikko-ikki inscription tiles is approximate; some sources propose Tenshō 8 ({{ill|1580 AD|ja|1580th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|July 5|ja|7月5日|12=draft|12=draft}} )). * {{ill|Kansei|ja|Kansei|12=draft|12=draft}} 2nd Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1790 AD|ja|1790th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|July 6|ja|7月6日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The Kansei Prohibition on Heterodox Learning was enacted, with {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信|12=draft|12=draft}} banning the teaching of subjects other than {{ill|Neo-Confucianism|ja|朱子学|12=draft|12=draft}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂|12=draft|12=draft}} .
== Births == * {{ill|Tenryaku|ja|天暦|12=draft|12=draft}} 4th Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|950 AD|ja|950th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 12|ja|6月12日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Emperor Reisō|ja|冷泉天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} , the 63rd {{ill|Emperor|ja|天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (d. {{ill|1011 AD|ja|1011th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Gennin|ja|元永|12=draft|12=draft}} 1st Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1118 AD|ja|1118th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Chiba Tsunetane|ja|千葉常胤|12=draft|12=draft}} , a military commander and protector (shugo) of {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国|lt=Shimōsa|12=draft|12=draft}} (d. {{ill|1201 AD|ja|1201st year|12=draft|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Shōhō|ja|正保|12=draft|12=draft}} 1st Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1644 AD|ja|1644th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 28|ja|6月28日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunashige|ja|徳川綱重|12=draft|12=draft}} , the first lord of the {{ill|Kōfu Domain|ja|甲府藩|12=draft|12=draft}} , the third son of {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光|12=draft|12=draft}} , and the father of {{ill|Tokugawa Ienobu|ja|徳川家宣|12=draft|12=draft}} (d. {{ill|1678 AD|ja|1678th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Kōka|ja|弘化|12=draft|12=draft}} 3rd Year (leap May) ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1846 AD|ja|1846th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa Iemoto|ja|徳川家茂|12=draft|12=draft}} , the 14th shogun of the {{ill|Edo Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|12=draft|12=draft}} (d. {{ill|1866 AD|ja|1866th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} 4th Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1871 AD|ja|1871st year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|July 11|ja|7月11日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉|12=draft|12=draft}} , a historian (d. {{ill|1939 AD|ja|1939th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 13th Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1636 AD|ja|1636th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 27|ja|6月27日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Date Masamune|ja|伊達政宗|12=draft|12=draft}} , a military commander and the first lord of the {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩|12=draft|12=draft}} (born {{ill|1567 AD|ja|1567th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== Commemorations == *

[[sn:May 24 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 5월 24일]] [[ja:5月24日 (旧暦)]] [[zh:五月廿四]] [[en:draft:May 24 (Lunisolar)]] [[ja:年表]] [[sn:May 24日 (旧暦)]] [[sn:ay 24 (Lunisolar)]] [[ar:May 24 (Lunisolar)]]

[[Category:pages to sort]][[cdo:5 nguŏk 24 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 24 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月廿四]]
{{Lunisolar May}} '''May 24''' is the 24th day of month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} day is {{ill|Butsumetsu|ja|仏滅|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} .
== Events == * {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 3rd Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|699 AD|ja|699th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 26|ja|6月26日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The founder of Shugendō, {{ill|En no Ozunu|ja|役行者|lt=En no Ozunu|lt_ja=役小角|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , was exiled to {{ill|Izu Province|ja|Izu Province|lt=Izu|lt_ja=伊豆|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} due to slander from his disciples. * {{ill|Bunji|ja|文治|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 1st Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1185 AD|ja|1185th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – To appease {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|lt=Yoshitsune|lt_ja=義経|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , who was en route to Kamakura, was detained at {{ill|Koshigoe|ja|腰越|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , and thus entrusted a letter (the {{ill|Koshigoe Letter|ja|腰越状|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) to {{ill|Ōe Hiromoto|ja|大江広元|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} . * {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 4th Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1576 AD|ja|1576th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Maeda Toshiie|ja|Maeda Toshiie|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} executed approximately 1,000 captives of the {{ill|Echizen Ikko-ikki|ja|越前一向一揆|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , subjecting them to crucifixion and boiling. (Dating based on Ikko-ikki inscription tiles is approximate; some sources propose Tenshō 8 ({{ill|1580 AD|ja|1580th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|July 5|ja|7月5日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} )). * {{ill|Kansei|ja|Kansei|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 2nd Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1790 AD|ja|1790th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|July 6|ja|7月6日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The Kansei Prohibition on Heterodox Learning was enacted, with {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} banning the teaching of subjects other than {{ill|Neo-Confucianism|ja|朱子学|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} .
== Births == * {{ill|Tenryaku|ja|天暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 4th Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|950 AD|ja|950th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 12|ja|6月12日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Emperor Reisō|ja|冷泉天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , the 63rd {{ill|Emperor|ja|天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (d. {{ill|1011 AD|ja|1011th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Gennin|ja|元永|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 1st Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1118 AD|ja|1118th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Chiba Tsunetane|ja|千葉常胤|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , a military commander and protector (shugo) of {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国|lt=Shimōsa|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (d. {{ill|1201 AD|ja|1201st year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Shōhō|ja|正保|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 1st Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1644 AD|ja|1644th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 28|ja|6月28日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunashige|ja|徳川綱重|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , the first lord of the {{ill|Kōfu Domain|ja|甲府藩|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , the third son of {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , and the father of {{ill|Tokugawa Ienobu|ja|徳川家宣|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (d. {{ill|1678 AD|ja|1678th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Kōka|ja|弘化|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 3rd Year (leap May) ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1846 AD|ja|1846th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa Iemoto|ja|徳川家茂|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , the 14th shogun of the {{ill|Edo Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (d. {{ill|1866 AD|ja|1866th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 4th Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1871 AD|ja|1871st year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|July 11|ja|7月11日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , a historian (d. {{ill|1939 AD|ja|1939th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 13th Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1636 AD|ja|1636th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 27|ja|6月27日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Date Masamune|ja|伊達政宗|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , a military commander and the first lord of the {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (born {{ill|1567 AD|ja|1567th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).

year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Gennin|ja|元永|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 1st Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1118 AD|ja|1118th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Chiba Tsunetane|ja|千葉常胤|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , a military commander and protector (shugo) of {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国|lt=Shimōsa|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (d. {{ill|1201 AD|ja|1201st year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Shōhō|ja|正保|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 1st Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1644 AD|ja|1644th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 28|ja|6月28日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunashige|ja|徳川綱重|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , the first lord of the {{ill|Kōfu Domain|ja|甲府藩|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , the third son of {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , and the father of {{ill|Tokugawa Ienobu|ja|徳川家宣|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (d. {{ill|1678 AD|ja|1678th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Kōka|ja|弘化|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 3rd Year (leap May) ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1846 AD|ja|1846th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|July [[Category:Pages to sort]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:May 24 (Lunisolar)}}[[Category:qq]] [[cdo:5 nguŏk 24 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[Category:Pages to sort]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:May 24 (Lunisolar)}}[[Category:qq]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 24 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[Category:Pages to sort]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:May 24 (Lunisolar)}}[[Category:qq]] [[zh-yue:五月廿四]]
{{Lunisolar May}} '''May 24''' is the 24th day of month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} day is {{ill|Butsumetsu|ja|仏滅|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} .
== Events == * {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 3rd Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|699 AD|ja|699th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 26|ja|6月26日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The founder of Shugendō, {{ill|En no Ozunu|ja|役行者|lt=En no Ozunu|lt_ja=役小角|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , was exiled to {{ill|Izu Province|ja|Izu Province|lt=Izu|lt_ja=伊豆|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} due to slander from his disciples. * {{ill|Bunji|ja|文治|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 1st Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1185 AD|ja|1185th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – To appease {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|lt=Yoshitsune|lt_ja=義経|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , who was en route to Kamakura, was detained at {{ill|Koshigoe|ja|腰越|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , and thus entrusted a letter (the {{ill|Koshigoe Letter|ja|腰越状|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) to {{ill|Ōe Hiromoto|ja|大江広元|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} . * {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 4th Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1576 AD|ja|1576th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Maeda Toshiie|ja|Maeda Toshiie|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} executed approximately 1,000 captives of the {{ill|Echizen Ikko-ikki|ja|越前一向一揆|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , subjecting them to crucifixion and boiling. (Dating based on Ikko-ikki inscription tiles is approximate; some sources propose Tenshō 8 ({{ill|1580 AD|ja|1580th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|July 5|ja|7月5日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} )). * {{ill|Kansei|ja|Kansei|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 2nd Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1790 AD|ja|1790th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|July 6|ja|7月6日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The Kansei Prohibition on Heterodox Learning was enacted, with {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} banning the teaching of subjects other than {{ill|Neo-Confucianism|ja|朱子学|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} at {{ill|Yushima Seidō|ja|湯島聖堂|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} .
== Births == * {{ill|Tenryaku|ja|天暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 4th Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|950 AD|ja|950th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 12|ja|6月12日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Emperor Reisō|ja|冷泉天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , the 63rd {{ill|Emperor|ja|天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (d. {{ill|1011 AD|ja|1011th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Gennin|ja|元永|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 1st Year ( {{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1118 AD|ja|1118th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Chiba Tsunetane|ja|千葉常胤|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , a military commander and protector (shugo) of {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国|lt=Shimōsa|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (d. {{ill|1201 AD|ja|1201st year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Shōhō|ja|正保|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 1st Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1644 AD|ja|1644th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 28|ja|6月28日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunashige|ja|徳川綱重|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , the first lord of the {{ill|Kōfu Domain|ja|甲府藩|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , the third son of {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , and the father of {{ill|Tokugawa Ienobu|ja|徳川家宣|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (d. {{ill|1678 AD|ja|1678th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Kōka|ja|弘化|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 3rd Year (leap May) ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1846 AD|ja|1846th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa Iemoto|ja|徳川家茂|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , the 14th shogun of the {{ill|Edo Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (d. {{ill|1866 AD|ja|1866th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 4th Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1871 AD|ja|1871st year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|July 11|ja|7月11日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , a historian (d. {{ill|1939 AD|ja|1939th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 13th Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1636 AD|ja|1636th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 27|ja|6月27日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Date Masamune|ja|伊達政宗|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , a military commander and the first lord of the {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (born {{ill|1567 AD|ja|1567th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== Commemorations == *
